title: Contrastive coordination for sparse features
learningObjectives: Explain contrastive coordination for sparse features in plain language
Write down the objective and identify its assumptions
Design one test that could falsify an over-strong interpretation

whyThisLesson.text: For SetConCA, a coordination loss should encourage related views to use compatible sparse codes without making all features identical. This lesson gives you a concrete test for that claim before the course moves to the next abstraction.

intuition.analogy: Contrastive learning is a sorting game: place genuine variants of the same item close together without piling every item into one corner. For SetConCA, a coordination loss should encourage related views to use compatible sparse codes without making all features identical.
intuition.analogyLimit: A lower contrastive loss can come from a shortcut in the views, not from recovery of the intended invariant.
intuition.text: For SetConCA, a coordination loss should encourage related views to use compatible sparse codes without making all features identical.

toyExample.description: Give each item two legitimate views and several near-duplicate negatives. Sweep temperature and inspect when the loss starts punishing semantically valid neighbours.

steps.result: Specify exactly what contrastive coordination for sparse features should recover, preserve, or ignore.
steps.insight: The intended factor must be known before a toy result can be judged.

steps.result: Use the same data, capacity, training budget, and evaluation examples for both methods.
steps.insight: Otherwise a visible gain may be a resource difference in disguise.

steps.result: Vary the source correlation, noise, view quality, or sparsity and record the first failure.
steps.insight: The boundary of success is usually more useful than the headline score.

visual.question: Which assumption behind contrastive coordination for sparse features changes the result most?
visual.description: Move one control at a time. Treat the display as a toy model, then write down the real experiment it suggests.

technical.text: For SetConCA, a coordination loss should encourage related views to use compatible sparse codes without making all features identical. In practice, write down the input unit, the operation applied to it, and the exact statistic used to score the result. Those are different things. A learned representation can be useful even when the interpretation attached to it turns out to be wrong.

mathematics.explanation: For this lesson, the expression makes the central trade-off explicit. For SetConCA, a coordination loss should encourage related views to use compatible sparse codes without making all features identical.
mathematics.whyNeeded: It says precisely what the method is being rewarded for.
mathematics.ifRemoved: You could not tell whether two implementations were solving the same problem.
mathematics.encourages: A testable prediction about the chosen objective.
mathematics.doesNotGuarantee: A lower contrastive loss can come from a shortcut in the views, not from recovery of the intended invariant.

methodComparison.question: What prevents collapse?
methodComparison.practicalImplication: The positive-pair definition is usually the highest-leverage choice.

evidence.claim: For SetConCA, a coordination loss should encourage related views to use compatible sparse codes without making all features identical.
evidence.note: Course explanation. Use the research library's primary sources for paper-specific empirical claims.

failureModes.name: A score that answers the wrong question
failureModes.mechanism: The objective rewards a proxy while the paper describes a stronger scientific property.
failureModes.symptom: The headline metric rises, but seed stability, controls, or interventions disagree.
failureModes.diagnosticTest: Add a matched negative control and one counterfactual intervention.
failureModes.mitigation: Either narrow the claim or add the missing measurement.
failureModes.setconcaRelevance: Report both the intended benefit and the most plausible shortcut for every SetConCA loss term.

setconcaConnection.relevance: Contrastive coordination for sparse features determines how SetConCA should represent, group, or evaluate activation views.
setconcaConnection.whichPart: The choice of sparse code, view set, coordination loss, or evaluation.
setconcaConnection.whichDesignChoice: Match the pointwise SAE and SetConCA on model, activation site, width, sparsity budget, and compute.
setconcaConnection.whichFailure: Mistaking stronger agreement for recovery of the intended concept.
setconcaConnection.whichExperiment: Compare fidelity, feature stability, selectivity, and controlled interventions; report each result separately.
setconcaConnection.alternativeExplanation: An apparent gain may come from extra context, effective capacity, or easier positives rather than set coordination.

exercises.prompt: A paper claims that contrastive coordination for sparse features improved interpretability. What one result would you ask for before accepting that claim?
exercises.reveal: Ask for the smallest missing control: a matched baseline, several seeds, a negative control, or an intervention that tests the claimed mechanism.

masteryQuestions.prompt: Explain contrastive coordination for sparse features to a colleague, then name one thing the method cannot establish by itself.

nextLesson.bridge: This phase checkpoint now combines the ideas from the phase.

fields.label: Falsifiable claim
fields.prompt: Write the smallest claim this lesson supports.

fields.label: Control
fields.prompt: What control would rule out a shortcut?
